name: Sissy Squat
resistance_profile: Bodyweight quad isolation with peak tension in the deep, knee-forward stretched position.
cues: Keep a straight line from knee to shoulder
Let the knees travel forward and lean back to counterbalance
Feel the work in the quads, not the knee joint
execution_steps: Stand tall, feet hip-width, holding a rack or pole for balance, and rise onto the balls of your feet.
Squeeze the glutes to keep your hips and torso in one straight line (hips extended).
Bend only at the knees, letting them travel forward as you lean your torso back to counterbalance.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch across the quads, keeping the knee-to-shoulder line straight.
Drive through the front of the thighs to extend the knees back to the standing start.
good_when: You want a knee-flexion-biased quad stretch without a machine
Training at home or with minimal equipment
bad_when: You have cranky or injured knees, since patellar load is high
You need heavy progressive overload, which is hard to add
You lack the balance or quad strength for the unsupported version
common_errors: Breaking at the hips instead of keeping them extended
Going deeper than the knees can currently tolerate
progressions: Hold a plate on the chest for added load
Reduce hand support and progress toward unassisted reps
regressions: Hold a solid support and assist with the arms
Limit the range to a comfortable knee angle
